  annual_quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual sales quota target assigned to the representative measured in base currency. Used for performance tracking and commission calculation.']
  book_of_business_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual recurring revenue or contract value of the customer accounts currently managed by this sales representative.']
  certification_list varchar [note: 'Professional certifications held by the sales representative relevant to industrial automation and electrification sales, such as product certifications, technical sales training, or industry credentials.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code for the sales representative used in order entry, commission tracking, and reporting systems.']
  commission_plan_code varchar [note: 'Reference code to the commission plan structure governing how this sales representative earns variable compensation based on sales performance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales representative record was first created in the sales system.']
  crm_user_code varchar [note: 'Salesforce Sales Cloud user identifier linking this sales representative to their CRM login and activity tracking.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Target customer segment that the sales representative primarily serves based on account size and strategic importance.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for the sales representative used for customer communication and internal correspondence.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the sales representative as recorded in the sales system.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date when the sales representative was hired into the sales organization.']
  industry_vertical_focus varchar [note: 'Target industry vertical or market segment that the sales representative focuses on, such as automotive, food and beverage, pharmaceuticals, or infrastructure.']
  is_key_account_manager boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this sales representative is designated as a key account manager responsible for strategic, high-value customer relationships.']
  language_proficiency varchar [note: 'Languages spoken by the sales representative relevant for customer communication and territory coverage, stored as comma-separated language codes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales representative record was most recently updated in the sales system.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review conducted for this sales representative.']
  last_training_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent sales training, product training, or professional development activity completed by the representative.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number for the sales representative used for field communication and urgent customer contact.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes capturing additional context about the sales representative\'s specializations, territory nuances, or other relevant information for sales operations.']
  onboarding_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the sales representative completed the formal onboarding and training program for new sales hires.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent performance evaluation rating for the sales representative based on quota attainment, customer satisfaction, and other Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number for the sales representative to be contacted by customers and internal stakeholders.']
  product_line_specialization varchar [note: 'Primary product line or solution area that the sales representative specializes in, such as drives, PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers), switchgear, building automation, or electrification systems.']
  quota_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the quota amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  quota_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the current quota period for this sales representative.']
  quota_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the current quota period for this sales representative, typically aligned with fiscal year or calendar year.']
  rep_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sales representative indicating their availability and eligibility for sales assignments.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel through which the representative conducts business: direct to end customer, through channel partners, distributors, or Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) relationships.']
  sales_office_location varchar [note: 'Primary office location or branch where the sales representative is based for administrative and reporting purposes.']
  sales_role varchar [note: 'Commercial role classification indicating the sales representative\'s position in the sales organization hierarchy.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the sales representative\'s employment or sales role ended. Null for active representatives.']
  travel_percentage decimal(9,4) [note: 'Expected percentage of work time spent traveling for customer visits, trade shows, and field sales activities.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total years of sales experience in industrial automation, electrification, or related technical sales roles.']

  Note: 'Sales representative profile within the sales domain capturing commercial role, quota assignment, product line specialization (drives, PLCs, switchgear, building automation), territory alignment, and performance targets. Distinct from the workforce domain employee record — this is the sales-domain view of a seller including their book of business, commission plan reference, CRM user linkage, and sales hierarchy position (individual contributor, team lead, regional manager). Sourced from Salesforce User / Sales Cloud.'
}

Table sales.price_book {
  price_book_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price book record. Primary key.']
  parent_price_book_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent or master price book from which this price book inherits base pricing. Null if this is a top-level price book.']
  product_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Product-family-scoped pricing governance: manufacturing price books are structured around product families (e.g., motors, drives, sensors). Direct FK to product.family replaces the denormalized produc']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: In manufacturing pricing governance, price books are scoped to customer segments (e.g., OEM vs. distributor vs. direct). Pricing analysts and commercial managers need to determine which price book app']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the price book was approved for use by authorized personnel.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether quotes or orders using this price book require management approval before finalization.']
  code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the price book for system integration and reporting purposes.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the specific country for which this price book applies. Null if applicable to multiple countries.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price book record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all prices in this price book (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY).']
  description decimal(18,2) [note: 'Detailed description of the price book purpose, scope, and applicability (e.g., \'Standard list prices for North America industrial automation products effective Q1 2024\').']
  discount_policy decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reference to the discount policy or guidelines that govern allowable discounts from this price book (e.g., \'Standard 10% max\', \'Volume-based tiered\', \'No discounts allowed\').']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the price book is no longer valid for new transactions. Null indicates no expiration date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the price book becomes valid and can be used for pricing sales transactions.']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'Specific industry vertical or segment for which this price book is designed (e.g., \'Automotive Manufacturing\', \'Food & Beverage\', \'Pharmaceuticals\', \'Energy & Utilities\').']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the price book is currently active and available for selection in sales opportunities and quotes.']
  is_standard boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the standard (default) price book for the organization. Only one standard price book should be active at a time.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price book record was last modified or updated.']
  name decimal(18,2) [note: 'Business name of the price book (e.g., \'Standard Price Book\', \'EMEA Industrial Automation 2024\', \'OEM Partner Pricing\').']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the price book usage and applicability.']
  price_book_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price book indicating whether it is available for use in sales transactions.']
  price_book_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification of the price book indicating its purpose and usage context within the sales organization.']
  pricing_strategy decimal(9,4) [note: 'The pricing methodology or strategy applied in this price book (e.g., list pricing, cost-plus, competitive pricing, value-based pricing).']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or territory for which this price book applies (e.g., \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\', \'LATAM\').']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current version of the price book was last revised or updated.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this price book in the source system for traceability and data lineage purposes.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the price book to track revisions and updates over time (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'2024.Q1\', \'Rev 3\').']

  Note: 'Commercial price book defining the standard or customer-specific list prices for industrial automation products, electrification solutions, and smart infrastructure components. Captures price book name, currency, effective date range, customer segment applicability, and whether it is the standard or a custom price book. Sourced from Salesforce CPQ Price Book. SSOT for sales-side pricing (distinct from product catalog cost data).'
}

Table sales.price_book_entry {
  price_book_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price book entry record. Primary key.']
  price_book_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent price book that contains this entry. Links to the price book master record defining the pricing context (standard, regional, promotional).']
  primary_price_catalog_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product or Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) that this price book entry applies to. Links to the product master record in the product catalog.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status for this price book entry. Tracks whether custom or non-standard pricing has been reviewed and approved per governance policies.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this price book entry was approved. Part of the audit trail for pricing governance and compliance.']
  cost_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The internal cost or standard cost of the product. Used for margin calculation and profitability analysis. Business-confidential data.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this price book entry record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which all prices in this entry are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The customer segment or tier for which this pricing applies (e.g., Enterprise, Mid-Market, Small Business, Government). Supports segment-based pricing strategies.']
  description decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional notes or description providing context about this pricing entry, such as special terms, conditions, or the rationale for custom pricing.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this price book entry is no longer valid. Null indicates no expiration. Used for promotional pricing and seasonal campaigns.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this price book entry becomes valid and can be used in sales transactions. Supports time-bound pricing strategies.']
  geography_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code indicating the geographic market for which this pricing applies (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price book entry is currently active and available for use in quotes and orders. Inactive entries are retained for historical reference.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this price book entry record was last modified. Part of the standard audit trail for change tracking.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The standard lead time in days from order placement to delivery for this product. Used for delivery date estimation in quotes and orders.']
  list_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The standard published list price for this product in the specified currency. Represents the baseline price before any discounts or negotiations.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'The target market segment or industry vertical for this product pricing (e.g., Automotive, Food & Beverage, Pharmaceuticals, Energy).']
  maximum_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable discount percentage that can be applied to this product without requiring special approval. Used to enforce pricing governance.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The minimum quantity that must be ordered for this product at this price. Enforces Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) policies in Configure Price Quote (CPQ) systems.']
  minimum_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The floor price below which this product cannot be sold. Used to enforce pricing policies and protect margin thresholds in Configure Price Quote (CPQ) processes.']
  name decimal(18,2) [note: 'A descriptive name or label for this price book entry, typically combining product name, price book name, and currency for easy identification in reports and user interfaces.']
  price_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'The type or nature of the price (e.g., list price, negotiated price, promotional price, contract price, spot price). Indicates the pricing strategy applied.']
  pricing_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to determine the price (e.g., fixed, cost-plus, market-based, value-based). Supports pricing strategy analysis and governance.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'The product family or category to which this product belongs (e.g., Automation Controllers, Electrification Components, SCADA Systems). Used for reporting and segmentation.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The business product line or division responsible for this product (e.g., Industrial Automation, Building Technologies, Mobility Solutions).']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which this pricing is applicable (e.g., direct sales, distributor, partner, Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM), online).']
  tax_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax classification code that determines applicable sales tax, Value Added Tax (VAT), or Goods and Services Tax (GST) rates for this product in the specified geography.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for pricing (e.g., Each, Box, Pallet, Kilogram, Meter). Defines the quantity basis for the unit price.']
  unit_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The effective selling price per unit for this product in the price book. May differ from list price due to volume discounts, promotions, or regional adjustments.']
  use_standard_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicates whether this entry uses the standard price book price or a custom price. True means standard pricing applies; false indicates custom or negotiated pricing.']

  Note: 'Individual product-price association within a price book, linking a specific SKU or product configuration to its list price, minimum price, and discount thresholds for a given currency and effective period. Supports multi-currency industrial sales across global markets and enables CPQ-driven automated pricing in Salesforce.'
}

Table sales.order_intake {
  order_intake_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for order_intake']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: When a customer order is booked, MRP/ERP production order creation requires the specific BOM revision to manufacture against. Order-to-BOM linkage is a fundamental manufacturing operations requirement']
  catalog_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_entry. Business justification: Order booking validation: order_intake must confirm the ordered item is an active, orderable catalog entry with correct pricing, lead time, and sales channel eligibility. Direct FK to catalog_entry en']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_contact. Business justification: Manufacturing order management requires tracking the customer contact (buyer/procurement officer) who placed or approved the order for dispute resolution, order confirmation communications, and audit ']
  credit_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Order-to-cash credit check at order booking references the customer\'s credit profile. order_intake has credit_check_status and credit_approval_date as plain attributes but no FK to the credit_profile ']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account placing the order. Links to the master customer account record.']
  delivery_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Order fulfillment in manufacturing requires a validated delivery address for logistics routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and hazmat compliance checks. order_intake.delivery_location is a plain-']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Order intake records the exact material to be delivered, linking to inventory for allocation and picking.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the won sales opportunity that generated this order intake. Links to the originating opportunity record in Salesforce Sales Cloud.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the accepted quote that was converted into this order intake. May be null if order originated directly from opportunity without formal quote.']
  revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Customer orders must be fulfilled to a specific engineering revision (as-ordered configuration) for warranty traceability, regulatory compliance, and field service. Capturing the revision at order boo']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master sales contract or framework agreement under which this order was placed. May be null for spot orders without framework agreement.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or account manager who owns this order intake. Used for quota attainment and commission calculation.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Order intake processing must map incoming orders to the master product record for inventory allocation and production scheduling.']
  booking_recognition_date date [note: 'The date when this order intake was recognized as a booking for quota and sales performance measurement. May differ from intake date based on validation rules.']
  booking_recognized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order intake has been recognized as a booking against sales quota. True when order meets booking recognition criteria per sales policy.']
  committed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date committed by sales to the customer. May differ from requested date based on production capacity and supply chain constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this order intake record was first created. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_approval_date date [note: 'Date when credit approval was granted for this order, if required. Null if order did not require credit approval or is still pending.']
  credit_check_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Result of the customer credit check performed at order intake. Orders failing credit check may require advance payment or credit approval before handoff to fulfillment.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order value. Essential for multi-currency sales operations and financial consolidation.']
  customer_po_date date [note: 'The date on the customer\'s purchase order. Used for contract compliance verification and order validity checks.']
  customer_po_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s purchase order reference number. Critical for order validation, invoicing, and customer reconciliation. May be required per customer contract terms.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied at intake date to convert order value to base currency. Locked at intake for consistent booking valuation.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter in which this order intake was booked. Format Q1-YYYY through Q4-YYYY. Used for quarterly sales performance reporting and quota tracking.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this order intake was booked. Used for annual sales performance reporting and year-over-year growth analysis.']
  handoff_date date [note: 'The date when the order intake was successfully handed off to the order management domain. Used for sales-to-fulfillment cycle time KPI calculation.']
  handoff_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the handoff from sales to order management domain. Tracks the intake record through validation, transfer, and acceptance by the fulfillment organization.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'The Incoterms rule defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Critical for international trade compliance and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'The customer\'s industry segment or vertical market. Used for market analysis, sales territory planning, and industry-specific KPI tracking.']
  intake_date date [note: 'The date when the order was officially received and recorded in the sales system. This is the booking date used for sales quota recognition and revenue pipeline tracking.']
  intake_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the order intake record. Human-readable unique number used for tracking and reference in sales reporting and KPI dashboards.']
  intake_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the order intake record was created in the system. Used for sales-to-fulfillment cycle time measurement and operational analytics.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this order intake record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this order intake record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments related to this order intake. May include customer requests, internal coordination notes, or special handling instructions.']
  order_management_reference varchar [note: 'The order number or reference assigned by the order management system after handoff. Links this sales intake record to the authoritative order record in SAP SD or equivalent ERP.']
  order_priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to this order intake. Influences production scheduling and resource allocation in the fulfillment process.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order by business type. Distinguishes between new customer acquisition, existing customer expansion, and service orders for sales mix analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: New Business|Renewal|Upgrade|Add-On|Replacement|Service|Spare Parts — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total confirmed order value at intake. This is the gross order amount before any adjustments, used for sales booking recognition and quota attainment calculation.']
  order_value_base_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Order value converted to the company\'s base reporting currency using the exchange rate at intake date. Used for consolidated sales reporting and KPI tracking.']
  payment_terms decimal(18,2) [note: 'The agreed payment terms for this order. Defines when payment is due relative to delivery or invoice date. Critical for cash flow forecasting and credit risk management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Net 30|Net 45|Net 60|Net 90|Advance Payment|Letter of Credit|Installment|Custom — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'Number of days until payment is due per the agreed payment terms. Used for accounts receivable forecasting and working capital planning.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The primary product line or business unit for this order. Used for sales performance segmentation and product portfolio analysis.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The customer\'s requested delivery date as captured at order intake. Used for production planning coordination and customer expectation management.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'The agreed shipping or transportation method for order delivery. Impacts delivery lead time and logistics cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Air Freight|Sea Freight|Road Transport|Rail|Courier|Customer Pickup|Multimodal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_terms_conditions varchar [note: 'Any special commercial terms, conditions, or customer-specific agreements applicable to this order. May include warranty extensions, service level commitments, or pricing exceptions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the sales user who created this order intake record in the system.']

  Note: 'Sales order intake record capturing the commercial handoff from a won opportunity or accepted quote to the order management domain. Records the intake date, confirmed order value, customer PO reference, requested delivery date, payment terms confirmed, and handoff status. This is NOT the SSOT for order fulfillment data — the authoritative order record lives in the order domain. The sales domain retains this record solely for order intake KPI tracking, sales-to-fulfillment cycle time measurement, and booking recognition against quota.'
}

Table order.header {
  header_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sales order header.']
  blanket_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Business justification: A blanket order (long-term scheduling agreement) generates individual release orders (order_headers). order_header should carry a FK back to the blanket_order it was released against, enabling traceab']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Order processing uses a sold‑to contact for invoicing and communication; this contact is stored in customer.contact.']
  credit_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Manufacturing O2C credit check at order entry requires recording which credit profile version was active at order placement — essential for audit trails, credit hold dispute resolution, and SOX compli']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who placed the order.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Revenue attribution: linking orders to the originating sales opportunity enables pipeline‑to‑revenue reporting and commission calculations.']
  order_intake_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Order Management traceability: each order must reference the intake record that created it for audit and fulfillment reporting.']
  price_book_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_book. Business justification: Manufacturing order pricing audit and revenue recognition require knowing which price book governed the order. The denormalized price_list column on order_header should be replaced by a proper FK to p']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: Project-based and engineer-to-order manufacturing requires linking sales orders to the governing engineering project for project cost tracking, revenue recognition against project budgets, and NPI ord']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Contract compliance: orders are executed under a sales contract; linking ensures legal and pricing terms are enforced per order.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Commission & accountability: each order is credited to the responsible sales rep, supporting commission payout and performance metrics.']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Shipping logistics require a reference to the customer\'s shipping address; address data resides in customer.address.']
  sla_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Individual sales orders in manufacturing execute under specific SLA agreements with committed lead times and penalty clauses. Linking order_header to sla_agreement supports on-time delivery KPI report']
  billing_block boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether billing of the order is blocked.']
  credit_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Credit check result for the customer at order time.']

  division varchar [note: 'Business division handling the order.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms governing freight cost responsibility (e.g., prepaid, collect).']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the order including packaging, measured in kilograms.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAP|DDP — promote to reference product]']
  internal_comments varchar [note: 'Internal notes visible only to company personnel.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total net weight of all items in the order, measured in kilograms.']

  order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the order as used in customer communications.']
  order_placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer placed the order.']
  order_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the order for processing.']
  order_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the order was created (e.g., new project, replacement).']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|partially_delivered|completed|cancelled|closed|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  order_text varchar [note: 'Long free‑text field for additional order instructions or remarks.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order based on fulfillment rules.']
  payment_terms decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual terms defining when payment is due (e.g., NET30).']
  price_group decimal(18,2) [note: 'Group determining price level for the customer.']
  pricing_date date [note: 'Date on which the pricing conditions were determined.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number supplied by the customer for their internal tracking.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order record was first captured in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the order record.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date requested by the customer for order delivery.']
  route varchar [note: 'Planned transportation route for delivering the order.']
  sales_document_type varchar [note: 'SAP SD document type code (e.g., OR for standard order).']
  sales_group varchar [note: 'Group of sales representatives responsible for the order.']
  sales_office varchar [note: 'Geographic sales office handling the order.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'Code of the sales organization responsible for the order.']
  shipping_condition varchar [note: 'Condition governing the shipping method for the order.']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Logistics location from which the order will be shipped.']
  tax_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax code applied to the order for tax calculation.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of line item amounts before taxes, discounts, and surcharges.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes, discounts, and surcharges.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the order.']
  transportation_group varchar [note: 'Classification of transportation mode and carrier.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the order in cubic meters.']

  Note: 'Core master record for customer purchase orders representing the full order commitment in the order-to-cash lifecycle. Captures customer reference, order type (standard, rush, blanket, consignment), requested delivery date, incoterms, payment terms, pricing date, total net value, currency, sales organization, distribution channel, division, and overall order status. Serves as the SSOT for all customer order commitments in the industrial manufacturing order lifecycle, driving downstream delivery, billing, and revenue recognition.'
}

Table order.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each order line record.']
  blanket_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Adding blanket_order_id as a']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: Enables Production Planning to pull the exact BOM version for the ordered product, essential for material requirement planning (MRP) reports.']
  catalog_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_entry. Business justification: Sales order lines in manufacturing are placed against a specific catalog entry (the priced, orderable item) to drive pricing conditions, lead time, and configurability. A manufacturing ERP expert expe']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Required for Order‑to‑Manufacturing handoff report linking each sold line to the engineered component for traceability and production scheduling.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent sales order (transaction header) to which this line belongs.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Order fulfillment requires linking each order line to the material master for inventory reservation, costing, and MRP planning; this is standard in manufacturing ERP systems.']
  price_book_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_book_entry. Business justification: Each manufacturing order line is priced from a specific price book entry. This FK is essential for pricing traceability, margin analysis, and revenue recognition audits — a domain expert would expect ']
  quote_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Audit trail: order line must reference the originating quote line to validate pricing, configuration, and warranty obligations.']
  revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: In engineer-to-order and configure-to-order manufacturing, an order line commits to a specific engineering revision for production planning, PPAP compliance, and customer-approved configuration manage']
  ship_to_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Multi-site manufacturing customers require line-level ship-to addresses — different order lines ship to different plant locations. This FK supports split-shipment planning, site-specific delivery sche']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Required for Order Fulfillment & Costing report linking each order line to the master product record for pricing, compliance, and warranty tracking.']
  spare_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.spare_part. Business justification: Needed for Spare Part Sales process to tie sold line items to the spare‑part master for warranty and inventory tracking.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Picking process assigns a specific storage location to each order line to locate inventory in the warehouse; the link enables pick list generation and inventory accuracy.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the goods were actually received by the customer.']
  backorder_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the line is on backorder, otherwise false.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for traceability of the material.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the system after availability check.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order line record was created in the source system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values on this line.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date the line was delivered to the customer.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the line\'s delivery.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to this line.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the product is sold (e.g., online, direct).']
  division varchar [note: 'Business division responsible for the product.']
  gross_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total price after taxes and before discounts.']
  gross_weight decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total weight of the line item including packaging (kilograms).']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection process.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned lead time in days from order to delivery.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the order, used for ordering and reference.']
  net_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net price per unit before taxes and discounts.']
  net_weight decimal(15,3) [note: 'Weight of the product itself without packaging (kilograms).']
  plant varchar [note: 'SAP plant code where the product is stocked or produced for this order line.']
  pricing_condition varchar [note: 'Pricing condition type governing the price calculation.']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the product or service on the line.']
  promised_date date [note: 'Date promised to the customer for delivery.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing quality assessment (0‑100).']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of quality inspection for the line item.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided when the line is rejected or cancelled.']
  requested_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity originally requested by the customer.']
  rma_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to an RMA record if the line is a return.']
  sales_org varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the sale.']
  sales_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Price per unit used for revenue recognition.']
  sales_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity used for sales reporting, may differ from requested/confirmed units.']
  schedule_line_date date [note: 'Planned date for delivery or production of this line item.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number for serialized items.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or bin location from which the material will be shipped.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount applicable to this line item.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., each, kilogram).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the order line record.']
  volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'Physical volume of the line item (cubic meters).']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for volume.']
  weight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for weight fields.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a customer sales order representing a discrete product, automation system, or service being ordered. Captures material number, ordered and confirmed quantities, unit of measure, schedule line dates, net price, pricing conditions, plant assignment, storage location, delivery status, rejection reason, and blanket order release reference. Serves as the demand signal for MRP and production scheduling. May reference a parent blanket order for scheduling agreement releases.'
}

Table order.schedule_line {
  schedule_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the schedule line record.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: A schedule line defines confirmed delivery quantities and dates, and is the direct precursor to an outbound delivery document in SAP SD. Linking schedule_line.delivery_id -> order.delivery.delivery_id']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent sales order header to which this schedule line belongs.']
  plant_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plant_data. Business justification: Schedule line ATP confirmation and MRP scheduling in manufacturing directly depend on plant_data parameters (MRP type, lot size, safety stock, planned delivery time) for the specific SKU-plant combina']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or material being scheduled for delivery.']
  split_parent_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original schedule line when this line is a split part.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Production scheduling allocates order schedule lines to exact stock locations for material staging; linking supports real-time allocation and capacity planning.']
  backorder_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the schedule line is backordered due to insufficient stock.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for traceability of the produced material.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Date confirmed by the system for delivery after scheduling.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the system after ATP check and production planning.']
  confirmed_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the confirmed quantity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the line amount.']
  goods_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the goods were posted to inventory for shipment.']
  handling_unit varchar [note: 'Identifier of the handling unit (e.g., pallet, container) used for the shipment.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing delivery responsibilities.']
  line_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value of the confirmed quantity before taxes.']
  mrp_confirmed_availability_date date [note: 'Date when material availability was confirmed by MRP.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority of the schedule line for production and delivery planning.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer originally requested for delivery.']
  requested_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity originally requested by the customer for this schedule line.']
  requested_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the requested quantity (e.g., PCS, KG, M3).']
  route varchar [note: 'Planned transportation route for the delivery.']
  schedule_line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the schedule line within the order line item.']
  schedule_line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the schedule line.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number for serialized items in the schedule line.']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Logistics location from which the goods will be shipped.']
  split_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the original order line has been split into multiple schedule lines.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse location where the goods will be staged before shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule line record.']

  Note: 'Delivery schedule line within a sales order line item defining confirmed delivery quantities and dates for industrial manufacturing fulfillment. Captures schedule line number, confirmed quantity, delivery date, goods issue date, route, shipping point, and MRP-confirmed availability date. Critical for Available-to-Promise (ATP) checks and production scheduling alignment in the order fulfillment process.'
}

Table order.delivery {
  delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for delivery']
  delivery_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who placed the original sales order.']
  delivery_ship_to_party_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party to which the goods are shipped.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Link delivery to its originating order header; enables traceability and removes redundant order number fields.']
  ship_to_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Carrier routing, customs compliance, and logistics planning require resolving the validated ship-to address on each delivery. Denormalized address fields on delivery violate 3NF; linking to customer.a']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Outbound deliveries originate from a specific warehouse; linking delivery to warehouse enables warehouse-level outbound volume reporting, dock scheduling, and capacity planning. Manufacturing operatio']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the delivery was actually received by the customer.']
  actual_goods_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when goods were actually issued from the warehouse.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the logistics carrier responsible for transportation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'External delivery document number assigned by SAP for tracking and communication.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery.']
  delivery_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery, e.g., stock shipment, return, consignment.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base freight charge before taxes and surcharges.']
  freight_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the freight cost.']
  freight_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight charge including taxes and surcharges.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Special handling notes for the carrier (e.g., fragile, keep upright).']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery contains hazardous or regulated materials.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'True when the delivery contains items that were previously on backorder.']
  is_partial_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery fulfills only part of the sales order quantity.']
  number_of_items varchar [note: 'Count of individual line items included in the delivery.']
  planned_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the delivery is scheduled to arrive at the customer location.']
  planned_goods_issue_date date [note: 'Planned date on which goods are to be issued from inventory.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the delivery for scheduling purposes.']
  shipping_condition varchar [note: 'Incoterm defining responsibility and cost allocation between seller and buyer.']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Plant or warehouse location code where the delivery originates.']
  special_equipment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if special equipment (e.g., liftgate) is needed for delivery.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'True when the shipment must be kept within a temperature range.']
  total_gross_weight_kg decimal(18,2) [note: 'Combined gross weight of all items in the delivery, expressed in kilograms.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Combined volume of the delivery items, expressed in cubic meters.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier provided by the carrier to track the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the delivery record.']
  window_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the agreed delivery time window.']
  window_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the agreed delivery time window.']

  Note: 'Outbound delivery document created from a sales order authorizing the physical shipment of finished goods or automation systems from a plant or warehouse. Captures delivery number, delivery type, shipping point, planned goods issue date, actual goods issue date, total weight, volume, carrier, tracking number, and delivery status. Links order fulfillment to warehouse execution and logistics operations.'
}

Table order.delivery_item {

  revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: As-shipped configuration records must capture the exact engineering revision delivered. Required for CE marking compliance, RoHS/REACH regulatory traceability, warranty management, and field service. ']
  schedule_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.schedule_line. Business justification: A delivery item fulfills a specific schedule line — the confirmed delivery quantity and date from the sales order. delivery_item currently has line_id (order line reference) but lacks a schedule_line_']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Enables Delivery Traceability and Warranty Claim process by tying each delivered item to its master product record.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Delivery execution tracks the source stock location of shipped items for traceability and logistics reporting.']

  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier responsible for transportation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery line record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for the delivery of this line item.']
  goods_movement_status varchar [note: 'Status of the goods issue transaction for this line.']
  handling_unit_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the handling unit (pallet, crate) containing the material.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Result of the quality inspection (e.g., pass, fail, rework).']
  inventory_management_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the line is subject to inventory management.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'Category defining the business purpose of the line (e.g., standard sale, return, consignment).']
  movement_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the goods movement, if applicable.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code that defines the kind of goods movement.']
  pallet_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pallet on which the line item is loaded.']
  picking_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the picking process for this line.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Plant where the material is stocked for this delivery.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer‑promised delivery date agreed in the sales order.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of quality inspection for the delivered material.']
  quantity_delivered decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of material that has been delivered (goods issue).']
  quantity_ordered decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material ordered for this delivery line.']
  quantity_picked decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material that has been physically picked from inventory.']
  route varchar [note: 'Defined transportation route for the delivery.']
  serial_number_end varchar [note: 'Ending serial number of the range allocated to this line.']
  serial_number_start varchar [note: 'Starting serial number of the range allocated to this line, when serial‑managed.']
  shipping_condition varchar [note: 'Condition under which the goods are shipped (e.g., standard, express).']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Logistics point from which the goods are shipped.']
  special_stock_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicator for special stock types such as project stock, vendor consignment, etc.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse storage location from which the material is picked.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit in which quantities are expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the delivery line record.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical volume of the line item in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the line item in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within an outbound delivery document specifying the material, quantity, batch, storage location, and picking status for each product being shipped. Captures delivery item number, material number, delivery quantity, picked quantity, batch number, serial number range, storage location, and goods movement status. Supports warehouse picking, packing, and goods issue execution.'
}

Table order.rma {
  rma_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the RMA record.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who initiated the return.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original sales order linked to this RMA.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product to be sent as a replacement, if applicable.']
  return_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Reverse logistics in manufacturing requires scheduling carrier pickup at the customer\'s validated return address. The return_address_id FK enables 3PL carrier booking, hazmat certification checks, and']
  revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: RMA processing requires identifying the exact engineering revision shipped to determine disposition (repair/replace/scrap), root cause analysis, and whether a design change is needed. As-shipped revis']
  actual_return_date date [note: 'Date the returned items were actually received.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the RMA.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RMA was approved.']
  authorized_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of items the system authorizes for return based on the original order.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier handling the return shipment.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit to be applied to the customer\'s account.']
  credit_memo_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a credit memo will be issued for this RMA.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  expected_return_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the returned items should arrive at the plant.']
  handling_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged for processing the return.']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the returned items must undergo quality inspection.']
  is_damaged boolean [note: 'True if the returned item was received with damage.']
  is_repairable boolean [note: 'True if the returned item can be repaired rather than replaced.']
  is_warranty_claim boolean [note: 'True if the return is processed under a warranty agreement.']
  is_wrong_item boolean [note: 'True if the returned item does not match the original order.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount after tax and fees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by service or sales staff.']
  order_rma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the RMA.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the RMA record.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount to be refunded to the customer before taxes and fees.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when an RMA is rejected.']
  replacement_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of replacement units to be shipped.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RMA was initially requested by the customer or service team.']
  return_plant varchar [note: 'Plant or warehouse code where the returned items are to be received.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing why the product is being returned.']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing details about the return reason.']
  returned_quantity varchar [note: 'Actual number of units received back from the customer.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible RMA number assigned by the order management system.']
  rma_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the RMA (e.g., warranty, non‑warranty, repair, replacement).']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method used to ship the returned product back to the plant.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the refund or credit.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking identifier provided by the carrier for the return shipment.']

  Note: 'Return Material Authorization record managing the end-to-end return process for defective, damaged, or incorrectly shipped industrial products and automation systems. Captures RMA number, originating sales order reference, return reason code (quality defect, shipping damage, wrong item, warranty claim), authorized return quantity, return plant, credit memo indicator, inspection requirement flag, and RMA status. Integrates with returns processing workflows and quality non-conformance reporting.'
}

  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.line. Business justification: An RMA line item represents the return of a specific original order line item. rma_line currently has order_header_id and order_rma_id but no direct FK to the originating order line. Adding line_id ->']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Returned goods in manufacturing must be traced to the originating lot/batch for quality disposition decisions, warranty analysis, and potential recall linkage. Manufacturing quality teams require this']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work order created for reworking the returned item.']

  revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: At the RMA line level, the specific engineering revision of the returned component drives disposition decisions — is this a known-defective revision? Is a replacement available at a newer revision? Re']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent RMA document to which this line belongs.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master.   condition_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the physical condition of the returned item (e.g., new, damaged, used).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RMA line record was created in the system.']

  inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned item must undergo quality inspection.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection for the returned item.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the returned material.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for additional comments or special handling instructions.']
  original_delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the original delivery was posted.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the returned material was received at the warehouse.']

  warranty_claim_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warranty claim associated with this return.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within an RMA document specifying the material, quantity, and disposition instructions for each returned product. Captures RMA line number, material number, returned quantity, original delivery reference, serial numbers, batch number, condition code, disposition action (scrap, rework, restock, replace), and credit value. Supports quality inspection routing and inventory reintegration decisions.'
}

Table order.goods_issue {
  goods_issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for goods_issue']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer receiving the goods.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: A goods_issue posting event is triggered by and directly associated with an outbound delivery document. goods_issue currently stores delivery_doc_number as a denormalized STRING reference. Adding deli']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Link goods issue to the order header for end‑to‑end tracking, removing redundant sales order number.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Goods issues are always executed against a specific material master record; this FK enables material-level goods issue reporting, valuation reconciliation, and MRP consumption analysis. material_numbe']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Goods issue postings in manufacturing are always tied to a specific SKU for inventory valuation, cost accounting (COGS), and MRP consumption recording. material_number is a denormalized text field. A ']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Goods issue transaction records the originating stock location, essential for inventory decrement, audit trails, and compliance reporting.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when the goods were actually delivered to the customer.']
  cost_center decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost center responsible for the goods issue cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for delivery to the customer.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'System‑calculated expected delivery date based on lead times.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to external system such as carrier tracking number.']
  goods_issue_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods issue.']
  handling_unit varchar [note: 'Identifier of the handling unit (e.g., pallet) used for the issue.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing delivery responsibilities.']
  inventory_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account for inventory posting.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the goods issue was generated automatically by a system.']
  issue_number varchar [note: 'External document number assigned to the goods issue.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code indicating the nature of the goods issue.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount after tax.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Plant where the goods issue originated.']
  posting_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the goods issue posting.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods issue was posted in the source system.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Profit center associated with the revenue from this issue.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order associated with the material movement, if applicable.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material issued.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text notes about the goods issue.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this record is a reversal of a previous goods issue.']
  route varchar [note: 'Planned transportation route for the shipment.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the issued item, if serialized.']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Logistics shipping point from which the goods are dispatched.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Storage location from which the goods were issued.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount applicable to the goods issue, if any.']
  total_value_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the issued goods at standard cost.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the issued quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']
  valuation_area varchar [note: 'Organizational area for inventory valuation.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Method used for inventory valuation of the issued material.']

  Note: 'Goods issue posting event recording the physical departure of finished goods, automation systems, or components from a plant or warehouse against an outbound delivery document. Captures goods issue document number, posting date, movement type (standard issue, reversal), plant, storage location, material document number, total value at cost, and goods issue status. Triggers inventory reduction, revenue recognition eligibility, COGS posting, and billing due list creation. Represents the legal transfer of custody from manufacturer to carrier.'
}

Table order.blanket_order {
  blanket_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the blanket order.']
  credit_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Blanket orders commit significant future credit exposure in manufacturing. Finance teams monitor total committed value against the customer\'s credit limit. Linking blanket_order to credit_profile enab']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the customer that owns the blanket agreement.']
  price_book_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_book. Business justification: Manufacturing blanket orders operate under a specific negotiated price book for the agreement duration. The denormalized pricing_conditions column should be replaced by a proper FK to price_book, enab']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: In manufacturing, blanket orders are the operational release mechanism under a master sales contract (frame agreement). Linking blanket_order to sales_contract is fundamental for contract utilization ']
  sla_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Blanket orders in manufacturing are governed by master SLA agreements defining delivery windows, penalty clauses, and on-time delivery targets. Linking blanket_order to sla_agreement enables automated']
  agreement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context or special notes about the blanket order.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business number assigned to the blanket agreement.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the blanket order received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the blanket order.']
  blanket_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the blanket order.']
  contract_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status of the agreement.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the agreement defining its purpose and governing rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the blanket order record was first created in the system.']
  cumulative_released_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity that has been released to the customer to date.']
  cumulative_released_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of all released quantities to date.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the agreement.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the products will be delivered (e.g., wholesale, direct).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the blanket agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the blanket agreement expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  is_jit_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement supports JIT delivery scheduling.']
  last_release_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent call‑off release.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Smallest quantity the customer may release in a single call‑off.']
  minimum_order_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity.']
  next_release_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled release based on the release frequency.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant responsible for fulfilling releases under the agreement.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the committed quantity (e.g., each, kilogram).']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region (e.g., NA, EU) applicable to the agreement.']
  release_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval at which the customer may release call‑offs against the blanket order.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the sales side of the agreement.']
  total_committed_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of product(s) the customer has committed to purchase over the agreement period.']
  total_committed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the total committed quantity at the agreed pricing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the blanket order record.']

  Note: 'Long-term supply agreement or scheduling agreement from an industrial customer committing to purchase a defined quantity of products over a specified period at agreed pricing. Captures blanket order number, customer account, validity start and end dates, total committed quantity, total committed value, release frequency, minimum order quantity (MOQ), pricing conditions, and cumulative released quantity to date. Contains individual release call-offs as line-level records with release number, release date, quantity, requested delivery date, cumulative released quantity, and release status. Supports demand planning, JIT delivery scheduling, and production scheduling for high-volume OEM customers.'
}

  billing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by the invoice.']

  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is exempt from tax.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the invoice.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']

  Note: 'Core billing document representing a formal demand for payment issued to a customer for product sales, service contracts, or project-based engagements. Captures invoice number, issue date, due date, billing period, total amount, tax amount, discount amount, currency, invoice status (draft, issued, partially_paid, paid, overdue, cancelled, disputed, written_off), invoice type (standard, credit_note, debit_note, proforma, self_billing), payment terms code, billing address, PO reference, contract reference, and source transaction reference. Serves as the SSOT for credit notes (returns, pricing corrections, quality disputes, volume rebate settlements) and debit notes (price adjustments, surcharges, underbilling corrections) via the invoice_type discriminator. Also supports self-billing/evaluated receipt settlement (ERS) for consignment and VMI arrangements. Managed in SAP S/4HANA SD/FI billing document types (VF01/VF04).'
}

  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'True if the line is exempt from tax, false otherwise.']
